heading: Enable Effective Fields
body:
Set the `use_effective_fields` argument to `true` in your resource definition for
the cluster as the following example shows:

```bash
  resource "mongodbatlas_advanced_cluster" "this" {
      project_id           = mongodbatlas_project.this.id
      name                 = var.cluster_name
      cluster_type         = var.cluster_type
      use_effective_fields = true
      replication_specs    = var.replication_specs
      tags                 = var.tags
    }
```

To learn more, see [Effective Fields Module Example](https://github.com/mongodb/terraform-provider-mongodbatlas/tree/master/examples/mongodbatlas_advanced_cluster/effective_fields).

> **Important:** If you don't enable effective fields, you must manually configure a
> `lifecycle.ignore_changes` block to prevent resource drift when auto-scaling
> occurs.  **We do not recommend this approach** . To learn more, see [Auto-Scaling](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/mongodb/mongodbatlas/latest/docs/resources/advanced_cluster#auto_scaling)
> in the Terraform documentation.
